ddls:
CREATE TABLE "images" (
  "image_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each image */,
  "seriesuid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the image series */,
  "image_type" TEXT /* Type of image (e.g., CT, MRI, PET scan) */,
  "coordX" REAL /* X-coordinate of the image */,
  "coordY" REAL /* Y-coordinate of the image */,
  "coordZ" REAL /* Z-coordinate of the image */,
  "diameter_mm" REAL /* Diameter of the image in mm */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient associated with the image */,
  "study_date" TEXT /* Date when the image was taken */,
  "image_size_kb" INTEGER /* Size of the image in kilobytes */,
  "compression_algorithm" TEXT /* Compression algorithm used for the image */,
  "image_format" TEXT /* Format of the image (e.g., DICOM, JPEG) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("image_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_images_seriesuid FOREIGN KEY ("seriesuid") REFERENCES series ("seriesuid"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_images_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_images_image_type FOREIGN KEY ("image_type") REFERENCES imaging_protocols ("image_type")
)

CREATE TABLE "series" (
  "series_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each series */,
  "seriesuid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the series */,
  "series_description" TEXT /* Description of the series */,
  "image_count" INTEGER /* Number of images in the series */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient associated with the series */,
  "study_date" TEXT /* Date when the series was taken */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the imaging protocol used for the series */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department that performed the series */,
  "image_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the images in the series (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("series_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_series_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_series_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES imaging_protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_series_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "patient_name" TEXT /* Name of the patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "medical_record_number" TEXT /* Medical record number of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact information of the patient */,
  "insurance_provider" TEXT /* Insurance provider of the patient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "imaging_protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol */,
  "protocol_description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol */,
  "image_type" TEXT /* Type of image (e.g., CT, MRI, PET scan) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department that created the protocol */,
  "protocol_version" TEXT /* Version of the protocol */,
  "protocol_effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the protocol became effective */,
  "protocol_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the protocol expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_imaging_protocols_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "department_head" TEXT /* Name of the department head */,
  "department_location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the department */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., radiologist, clinician, admin) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the user belongs to */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the hospital */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "image_annotations" (
  "annotation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each annotation */,
  "image_id" INTEGER /* ID of the image being annotated */,
  "annotation_text" TEXT /* Text of the annotation */,
  "annotation_date" TEXT /* Date the annotation was made */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the annotation */,
  "annotation_type" TEXT /* Type of annotation (e.g., text, marker) */,
  "annotation_coordinates" TEXT /* Coordinates of the annotation (e.g., JSON object) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("annotation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_image_annotations_image_id FOREIGN KEY ("image_id") REFERENCES images ("image_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_image_annotations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "image_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "image_id" INTEGER /* ID of the image being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the image */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the image was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the user belongs to */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_image_access_logs_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_image_access_logs_image_id FOREIGN KEY ("image_id") REFERENCES images ("image_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_image_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
